expectations.text: Asks what type of contribution this is (new method, efficiency, new problem formulation, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly presents options A/B/C/E with explanation of why distinction matters for evaluation design

expectations.text: Asks which specific baseline methods will be compared against
expectations.evidence: Identifies three required baseline types including IQL, TD3+BC, DT examples

expectations.text: Asks about specific benchmarks, datasets, or numerical evaluation targets
expectations.evidence: Asks which D4RL tasks/splits, how to measure sample efficiency, what a win looks like concretely

expectations.text: Does not produce a complete proposal draft without first asking clarifying questions
expectations.evidence: No draft produced in either round — all responses are targeted questions
